content: The orthographic variant spelling 'reflexion' (the older British English spelling of the concept of reflection taught in high school language curricula) can be repurposed as a naming label for a new class of LLM self-reflection mechanisms that explicitly retain 'old' erroneous reasoning trajectories as persistent dynamic memory, separate from refined updated reasoning paths. This distinct spelling acts as a mnemonic to separate vanilla LLM self-reflection from this new variant that leverages orthographic distinction to improve structured memory separation.
